solution: ```
from collections import deque
class Solution:
    def canFinish(self, numCourses: int, prerequisites: List[List[int]]) -> bool:
        graph = [[] for _ in range(numCourses)]
        indeg = [0] * numCourses
        for a, b in prerequisites:
            graph[b].append(a)
            indeg[a] += 1
        q = deque([i for i in range(numCourses) if indeg[i] == 0])
        taken = 0
        while q:
            node = q.popleft()
            taken += 1
            for nxt in graph[node]:
                indeg[nxt] -= 1
                if indeg[nxt] == 0:
                    q.append(nxt)
        return taken == numCourses
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(V + E)
space complexity: O(V + E)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Topological sort via Kahn’s BFS algorithm detects cycles in the directed graph of courses. If all nodes are processed (taken == numCourses) no cycle exists and all courses can be finished; otherwise a cycle blocks completion.
